app.schemas package

Submodules

app.schemas.collection_schemas module

The module defines Pydantic schemas for managing collections. Includes schemas for inserting, selecting, updating, deleting, and listing collections.

class app.schemas.collection_schemas.CollectionDeleteResponse(*, collection_id: int)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Pydantic schema for the response after deleting a collection entity. It includes the ID assigned to the deleted collection.

collection_id: int
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

model_fields: ClassVar[dict[str, FieldInfo]] = {'collection_id': FieldInfo(annotation=int, required=True)}

Metadata about the fields defined on the model, mapping of field names to [FieldInfo][pydantic.fields.FieldInfo].

This replaces Model.__fields__ from Pydantic V1.

class app.schemas.collection_schemas.CollectionInsertRequest(*, is_locked: bool, collection_name: Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=1), MaxLen(max_length=256)], collection_summary: Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=512)] = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Pydantic schema for creating a new collection entity. It requires the collection’s locked status, name, and optionally a collection summary.

collection_name: str
collection_summary: str | None
classmethod filter_collection_name(collection_name: str) → str[source]

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

Metadata about the fields defined on the model, mapping of field names to [FieldInfo][pydantic.fields.FieldInfo].

class app.schemas.collection_schemas.CollectionListRequest(*, user_id__eq: int | None = None, collection_name__ilike: str | None = None, is_locked__eq: bool | None = None, offset: Annotated[int, Ge(ge=0)], limit: Annotated[int, Ge(ge=1), Le(le=200)], order_by: Literal['id', 'created_date', 'updated_date', 'locked_date', 'is_locked', 'user_id', 'collection_name', 'documents_count'], order: Literal['asc', 'desc', 'rand'])[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Pydantic schema for requesting a list of collection entities. It allows optional filters for collection name, locked status, and various counts and sizes. Also includes pagination options (offset and limit), and ordering criteria.

collection_name__ilike: str | None
is_locked__eq: bool | None
limit: int
model_config: ClassVar[ConfigDict] = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

Metadata about the fields defined on the model, mapping of field names to [FieldInfo][pydantic.fields.FieldInfo].

This replaces Model.__fields__ from Pydantic V1.

offset: int
order: Literal['asc', 'desc', 'rand']
order_by: Literal['id', 'created_date', 'updated_date', 'locked_date', 'is_locked', 'user_id', 'collection_name', 'documents_count']

class app.schemas.collection_schemas.CollectionSelectResponse(*, id: int, created_date: int, updated_date: int, locked_date: int, user_id: int, user_name: str, is_locked: bool, collection_name: str, collection_summary: str | None = None, documents_count: int)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Pydantic schema for the response after retrieving a collection entity. It includes the collection’s ID, creation and update dates, user ID, locked status, name, summary, document count, and associated user details.

collection_name: str
collection_summary: str | None
created_date: int
documents_count: int

app.schemas.comment_schemas module

The module defines Pydantic schemas for managing comments. It includes schemas for inserting, selecting, updating, deleting, and listing comments.

app.schemas.revision_schemas module

The module defines Pydantic schemas for managing document revisions. Includes schemas for selecting, listing, and retrieving details of document revisions. These schemas support pagination, ordering, and filtering to retrieve specific revisions pased on criteria such as document ID, revision creation date, and other properties.

class app.schemas.revision_schemas.DocumentRevisionsRequest(*, offset: Annotated[int, Ge(ge=0)], limit: Annotated[int, Ge(ge=1), Le(le=200)], order_by: Literal['id', 'created_date'], order: Literal['asc', 'desc'])[source]

app.schemas.time_schemas module

app.schemas.user_schemas module

The module defines Pydantic schemas for managing users. It includes schemas for registration, login, multi-factor authentication, token retrieval and invalidation, role and profile updating, userpic management, password changes, and user listing.

class app.schemas.user_schemas.PasswordUpdateRequest(*, current_password: Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=6)], updated_password: Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=6)])[source]

class app.schemas.user_schemas.UserRegisterRequest(*, user_login: Annotated[str, _PydanticGeneralMetadata(pattern='^[a-zA-Z0-9]{2,40}$')], user_password: Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=6)], first_name: Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=2), MaxLen(max_length=40)], last_name: Annotated[str, MinLen(min_length=2), MaxLen(max_length=40)], user_caption: Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=40)] = None, user_contacts: Annotated[str | None, MaxLen(max_length=512)] = None)[source]
